notes: Reproduced: DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp occurs in Blazor WASM when using raw SkiaSharp without SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly or SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor. WASM requires static linking of native libraries via NativeFileReference MSBuild items, which are only provided by the NativeAssets.WebAssembly package. The main branch Blazor sample works because it uses Views.Blazor (which includes NativeAssets.WebAssembly). This appears to be by-design but the recurring reports suggest the packaging UX needs improvement.

reproductionSteps.description: Create Blazor WASM project with raw SkiaSharp (no Views.Blazor, no NativeAssets.WebAssembly)
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro3224 --framework net10.0 && cd Repro3224 && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.118.0-preview.2
reproductionSteps.output:
The template "Blazor WebAssembly Standalone App" was created successfully.
Restore succeeded.
warn : NU1603: SkiaSharp 3.118.0-preview.2 was not found. SkiaSharp 3.118.0-preview.2.3 was resolved instead.

Build succeeded with 5 warning(s).
Browser: Raster Canvas page renders correctly with "SkiaSharp" text visible on canvas. No console errors.

versionResults.notes: DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp at SKImageInfo..cctor() in Blazor WASM without NativeAssets.WebAssembly
Same DllNotFoundException. Latest stable has the same behavior.

The sample using Views.Blazor (which includes NativeAssets.WebAssembly) works correctly. Raw SkiaSharp without NativeAssets.WebAssembly still causes DllNotFoundException — the packaging behavior is unchanged on main.

actionability.reason: The DllNotFoundException is caused by missing SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly package reference. WASM requires explicit static linking setup. Adding NativeAssets.WebAssembly or using Views.Blazor resolves the issue. This is documented behavior but the recurring reports suggest the packaging UX could be improved.

workarounds: Add SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly as a direct PackageReference in the Blazor WASM app project
Use SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor instead of raw SkiaSharp (auto-includes NativeAssets.WebAssembly)
Ensure wasm-tools workload is installed: dotnet workload install wasm-tools
missingInfo: Confirmation from reporter whether adding NativeAssets.WebAssembly resolves the issue

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for reporting this! We were able to reproduce the `DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp` in Blazor WASM.

**Root cause:** In Blazor WebAssembly, native libraries like libSkiaSharp must be statically linked into `dotnet.wasm` at build time. This linking only happens when the `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly` package is referenced in the WASM application project. When using raw `SkiaSharp` (without `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor`), these native assets are not automatically included.

**Workaround:** Add `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly` as a direct package reference in your Blazor WASM application project:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly" Version="3.119.2" />
```

Alternatively, use `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor` which automatically includes the WASM native assets.

Also ensure the `wasm-tools` workload is installed:
```
dotnet workload install wasm-tools
```

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 3.118.0-preview.2.3 | ❌ Reproduced |
| 3.119.2 (latest) | ❌ Reproduced |
| main (with Views.Blazor) | ✅ Works |
